Warning text improved: when quota is known, the modal shows "N of Q quests remaining (including this one)" rather than just the remaining count; the crimson final-quest line now reads "will depart after this quest (last of Q)". Falls back to the older format when quota is unknown. SQL required this time: run add-contact-quest-quota-columns.sql to add quests_remaining and quest_quota integer columns to the party table — idempotent, safe to re-run, includes an optional backfill from a legacy meta blob if one exists.
Version 1.6917 — Completes the Contact-eligibility rollout. After v1.6915/6916 fixed the peg-slot level match and the questHardGateIssues topLevel computation, the Begin button was still disabled on a quest staffed by Javvox (a Lv 4 Contact) because regularQuestRosterStatus — the "at least one Level 1+ adventurer" gate — still filtered out Contacts via the raw !c.hireling check. The info card showed the contradictory state of "✓ Level 4+ adventurer" (from the already-fixed difficulty gate) alongside "Difficulty gate not met" (from this roster-status gate). The v1.6915 design note said roster-composition gates should stay strict, but that was wrong for Contacts: the narrative intent of those gates is "don't let a quest be staffed entirely by 50gp disposable hirelings," which Contacts are not. Four more sites now use _charCountsForLevelSlot: (a) regularQuestRosterStatus — Contacts qualify as the required Lv 1+ adventurer; (b) grindRosterStatus — Contacts qualify as the solo-adventurer path, but are NOT counted toward the "two hirelings pair" path (which is explicitly for 50gp disposable bodies); (c) grindReadiness available pool; (d) questDifficultyRequirementMet and (e) averageLevelForIndices for tier/avg-level calculations. Helper's inline policy note rewritten to reflect the unified rule: all "is this a proper adventurer" gates now treat Contacts as qualifying; only level-up-UI remains strict (_showLvUp correctly hides the Level Up button on Contacts since they're not upgradeable via player gold). No SQL change.
Version 1.6916 — Follow-up to v1.6915: Contacts now also count toward the difficulty gate and related party-level calculations. After v1.6915, a Lv 4 Contact like Javvox could be placed on a Lv 4+ peg slot (the slot border turned green), but questHardGateIssues still computed its topLevel with the raw !c.hireling filter and reported "Missing: Party level 4+ (highest: 0)" — blocking the Begin button. Four more sites updated to use _charCountsForLevelSlot: (a) questHardGateIssues topLevel loop, (b) its busyHighLevel loop (controls the "your highest eligible adventurer is on another quest" message), (c) partyMaxLevel (used for tier selection and reward-range display), (d) questCalcReward topLevel loop (level bonus in gold reward). The "⬆ Level Up" peg button's !c.hireling gate is kept as-is — Contacts aren't level-upable through the player-pays-gold UI, and since they carry hireling:true the existing check correctly hides the button. buildTestRoster also kept raw: test rosters never include Contacts. No SQL change.
Version 1.6915 — Two Contact-related changes. (1) Fix: capstone-reward NPC Contacts count toward level prereqs. Root cause: capstone NPCs are created with hireling:true, contact:true so they inherit hireling-style defaults (zero hire cost, no level-up upsell). But six level-prereq sites gated eligibility with a raw !c.hireling check, so a Lv 4 Contact like "Javvox" was rejected from a Lv 4+ peg slot despite clearly meeting the requirement. New _charCountsForLevelSlot(c) helper: true for non-hirelings AND for hireling:true + contact:true NPCs; false for standard hirelings (which still cap at Lv 0). Applied at six sites: qddCharCoversSlot peg match (the direct symptom), prereq-row level met check (assigned pool + full pool), info-card level met check (assigned + full pool), characterMeetsAssignmentLevel, characterCanMeetAssignmentLevelAfterOneLevel, and the "meets level" pill in the adventurer pick list. Deliberately unchanged: grindRosterStatus and regularQuestRosterStatus still use the raw !c.hireling check because those gates enforce "the squad has at least one real adventurer" — that's a roster-composition policy separate from the level question. (2) Feature: limited-contact Begin warning. Contacts with a tracked questsRemaining counter are consumed by each quest and depart when it hits zero (existing mechanic, v1.666). Previously a user could click Begin and not realize they were burning a contact's last slot. Now on Begin: if any peg-assigned character is a contact with questsRemaining > 0, a new ov-contact-warn modal surfaces with one row per limited contact showing "N quests remaining (including this one)" (or "will depart after this quest" in crimson if only 1 left), a per-contact ✕ Unassign button that clears them from all peg slots without committing, plus Cancel and Begin Anyway footer actions. confirmQuestAssignment gained a skipContactWarn parameter so Begin Anyway doesn't re-prompt on re-entry; the downtime-forfeit gate still runs normally. No SQL change: Contact data already carries contact:true + questsRemaining from the NPC contact editor.
Version 1.6914 — Fix: phantom empty Faith peg slot on the quest-assignment modal (e.g. "Cellar Savant" showed a ✝ FAITH slot with no deity named). Root cause: shape drift between the Quest Editor's quest-matrix chip-picker (writes pre.rel as a JSON array) and the rest of the codebase (expects a single string or ""). The main app's peg-slot guard at qddBuildCharSlots only defended against null/undefined/""; empty arrays slipped through because [] is truthy AND [] !== "", so a Faith slot was pushed with slot.value = []. The render coerced esc([]) to the empty string and produced a bordered slot with a label but no visible requirement. Same drift also broke the single-faith quests (Ravenous, Hope Springs Eternal, Cold Trail, Rune Corrupted) whose pre.rel was ["Faith Name"] instead of "Faith Name" \u2014 peg-fill checks like c.rel===slot.value compared a string to an array and never matched, making the slots un-fillable. Fix (3 layers): (1) qddBuildCharSlots at line 23355 now accepts both array and string shapes, skips blank/whitespace/non-string entries, and trims. (2) mergeCustomContent now normalizes pre.rel to a single string at merge time \u2014 so all 10+ downstream consumers (grindReadiness, confirm-path best-fit scoring, prereq row rendering, hireling suggestions, etc.) see a canonical shape without per-site patching. (3) Quest Editor (v1.679) now writes pre.rel as a string consistently \u2014 both the form save path and the questToRow serializer coerce to the first non-empty string on save, preventing new bad data. One-time SQL cleanup provided as normalize-rel-shape.sql: flattens existing array-shaped pre.rel values across all quest rows. Dry-run preview + commented UPDATE; idempotent, safe to re-run. After the client fixes alone, rendering works correctly regardless of DB shape; the SQL is optional tidy-up to canonicalize the stored data.
Version 1.6913 — Grind refactor: grinds are now a unified, cycle-independent list reached through the Quest Map. (1) Adventure screen stripped of grinds. The per-cycle adventure view no longer renders the ⚙ Grind Missions section, its auto-assign button, in-progress cards, or [data-grind-header] anchor. Per-cycle screens show only that cycle's quests. The dead pre-block auto-assign button tied to use-before-def _canAutoGrind/_grindAutoCost (never actually rendered due to var hoisting) was also removed. (2) New dedicated Grinds modal ov-grinds-list: summary band (clocked-in squads · eligible characters · in-progress count), auto-assign button, all grinds sorted in-progress → ready → stretch → overreach → reward desc. Owns its own 1s countdown tick (grindsCountdownTimer) while open; re-renders when a mission crosses zero. (3) Ten new harder grinds (g12\u2013g21) with sliding difficulty: rewards 45\u2013100 gp, enforced minLevel 2\u20136, enforced minSquadSize 2\u20134, progressive prereqs (single-skill \u2192 multi-skill + tools + languages). grindRosterStatus() signature extended with a second grindObj parameter (backward-compatible when omitted) and now rejects rosters under a grind's minSquadSize; all four callers updated (grindReadiness, auto-assign loop, and both confirm-path checks). The Grinds modal surfaces minLevel and minSquadSize in each card's prereq line alongside skills/tools/languages. (4) Click handlers rebound to document scope. gassign / gcollect / grind-auto branches moved out of #avcontent's click listener into a fresh document-scoped listener so they fire from the new modal. Each mutation also triggers renderGrindsList() when the modal is visible so the view stays current without a manual reopen; confirmQuestAssignment's grind path does the same. Auto-assign's character pool was widened to match the Grinds modal's eligibility rules (active cycle + clocked-in + unassigned), replacing the old active-cycle-only pool. (5) Entry points re-wired. Quest Map \u201cOpen Grinds\u201d button no longer navigates to the Adventure screen and scrolls to a now-missing anchor \u2014 it calls openGrindsList() and closes the map modal. The v1.6912 party-row free-chooser\u2019s Grind path does the same. No SQL change: new grind entries are static data on GRIND[], and pending grind entries continue to store only id \u2014 minLevel/minSquadSize are looked up from GRIND[] at check time.
Version 1.6912 — Two coordinated features. (1) Quest-Editor Real Estate deny rules consumer. The Quest Editor (v1.678) now lets writers deny specific buildings (or the whole catalog) per completed cycle via a realEstateDeny field on the cycle's data JSONB blob — no SQL change needed. Main app now honors it: realEstateCatalog(setId) takes a setId and filters the curated list against the cycle's deny rules. New helpers realEstateDenyForSet(setId) and realEstateAllDeniedForSet(setId). renderRealEstateCatalog shows a clear 🚫 "Real estate unavailable" notice when the whole cycle is denied. The 🏘 Real Estate button on Quest Map cycle cards is hidden when all real estate is denied AND no buildings have already been assigned to the cycle; if owned buildings exist, the button stays available so users can still view them. (2) Party-row free chooser. Clicking the dotted-underline "free" status on a squad roster row no longer jumps straight to the downtime modal — it now opens ov-free-choice, a compact picker with 🕯 Downtime and ⚙ Grind Mission options. Downtime routes to the existing startDowntimeForChar(ci) unchanged. Grind option is hard-gated by _charCanGrind(ci): eligible only when the character is in a squad currently clocked in to the grind shift OR not in any squad (unassigned/ad-hoc); when ineligible the Grind button is hidden and the modal message explains why (either grinds not yet unlocked, or squad not clocked in). Confirming Grind navigates to the Adventure screen and scrolls to the grind section via the existing [data-grind-header] anchor. Same pattern as Quest Map's "Open Grinds" button. Gate is re-checked at confirm time as defense-in-depth.
Version 1.6911 — Fix: abandoning an active quest cycle caused previously-completed cycles (e.g. Root & Branch 10/10) to visually regress to a partial count matching the abandoned cycle's progress (e.g. 3/10). Root cause: the abandon handler cleared qs.setId, qs.pending, and qs.grindPending but left qs.done, qs.repeatCounts, qs.revealed, and qs.assigned holding the abandoned cycle's indices. With setId=null, activeQuestSet() falls back to QUESTSETS[0] (Root & Branch) — so render code then evaluated the stale indices against a different set's quests. Because quest indices (0, 1, 2, …) collide across sets, the first N quests of the fallback set appeared falsely complete. The true progress for both cycles was still correct in setProgress[setId] — only the live qs.* arrays were corrupt, so the regression was cosmetic but persistent (survived save/reload). Previously latent because players rarely lingered in the no-active-cycle state between abandon and re-activate; v1.6910's Real Estate button and Grind Missions card on the Quest Map invited exactly that browsing pattern, exposing the bug. Fix (abandon handler): when isActive and we null qs.setId, also reset qs.done=[], qs.repeatCounts={}, qs.revealed=[], qs.assigned={}. The cycle's persisted progress in setProgress[setId] (written one line above by saveActiveSetProgress()) is untouched, so re-activating the same cycle via Quest Map restores the correct state through the normal loadSetProgress() path. Fix (self-heal): added healOrphanedQuestState() — runs at load time alongside sanitizeDowntimeKeys() and migrateArtificerToRunesmith() across all three load paths (local load(), code-login, and sbPull). If qs.setId is null/missing but any of done / repeatCounts / revealed / assigned hold entries, those live arrays are cleared. Idempotent; no-op when state is legitimate. Self-heals any save already corrupted by the old handler so existing users recover on first load without manual re-activation.
Version 1.6910 — Quest Map overhaul redirecting access to Grind Missions and repurposing completed ("old") cycles as real-estate destinations. Data model: cycleSquadMap() now recognizes a special '__grind__' key whose value is an array of squad names (multi-squad clock-in). All existing setId → string entries are unchanged; the stale-cleanup pass now dedupes the grind array and handles arrays vs strings per key. New helpers: grindSquadNames(), isSquadClockedInToGrinds(), clockInSquadToGrinds(), clockOutSquadFromGrinds(), availableSquadsForGrindClockIn(), grindsUnlocked() (gated on isSetCompleted('root-and-branch')), plus a split of squadIsLockedByCycle() into a narrow squadIsLockedByActiveCycle() (excludes grinds) and the original back-compat wrapper (still used by squad-manager roster gates, which correctly reject both cycle- and grind-locked squads). squadsAssignedToOtherCycles() now also excludes squads clocked into grinds, so cycle activation can't steal them. Quest Map card changes: (1) Grind Missions card is now interactive — renders clocked-in squad chips with ✕ clock-out buttons, an "➕ Add Squad" button (opens new ov-grind-clockin picker), and an "⚡ Open Grinds" button that navigates to the Adventure screen and scroll-targets the new data-grind-header anchor. Locked state shown when Root & Branch isn't complete. (2) Completed cycle cards get a 🏘 Real Estate button (opens new ov-cycle-realestate catalog modal) and a building-count badge in the header. (3) Completed-cycle expanded bodies now list assigned buildings inline. Real Estate catalog: new realEstateCatalog() returns a curated 10-item subset of BUILDINGS across 200–3000 gp price points (Storage Shed, Stables, Market Stall, Barracks, Smithy, Tavern, Greenhouse, Alchemist's Lab, Library, Safe House). Purchase flow uses goldSpend(), pushes an asset with meta.ownerType:'cycle' and meta.ownerRef:setId, then opens a pet-purchase-style ov-cre-name naming modal (Skip / Save / Enter-to-save). cycleAssignedBuildings(setId) enumerates them for display. Adventure screen gate widened: the grind section previously required doneCount ≥ totalQ on the active cycle; it now also renders when grindsUnlocked() && grindSquadNames().length > 0. The auto-assign eligibility pool unions active-cycle squad + every clocked-in grind squad + unassigned characters. Grind assignment modal: populateQuestSquadPicker() detects P4.grind and swaps in a grind-specific squad list (active cycle squad prefixed with ⚔, clocked-in grind squads prefixed with ⚙, plus a 🧭 Unassigned (ad-hoc) sentinel that bypasses the squad auto-check-and-lock). applyQuestSquadSelection() treats '__unassigned__' specially so all peg slots stay unlocked for ad-hoc picking. The gassign handler no longer force-disables the dropdown, and confirmQuestAssignment() now reads the dropdown value for the grind path (storing '' for ad-hoc) instead of blindly persisting activeCycleSquadName().
Version 1.6906 — Renamed the Artificer class to Runesmith across both character generators. Six sites updated in the main app (the DND.cls list, plus the class-keyed maps for skills, base stats, starting equipment, weapon proficiencies, and inventory) and two in the Quest Editor (class-stats map, class list). Because c.cls is used as a data key throughout — e.g. CLASS_SKILLS[c.cls], CLASS_STATS[c.cls] — a pure rename would have stranded any saved character with cls:'Artificer' (all their class-driven lookups would silently miss). Added migrateArtificerToRunesmith() that rewrites cls to 'Runesmith' on S.party, any pending capstone-NPC queue, and partyAssets.meta (both direct .cls and nested .npc.cls shapes). The migration is idempotent and runs from all three load paths alongside sanitizeDowntimeKeys() — local load(), code-login, and the main sbPull — so corrupted save data heals on first session open.
Version 1.6905 — Party-screen roster status was wrong for any character on a non-active quest cycle. Root cause: three independent scans (questBusyForChar, the questingChars/downtimeChars map at the top of renderParty, and the _msUntilFree helper inside each squad panel) only read S.questState.pending and S.questState.grindPending — which hold the active cycle's live state only. Per the v1.551/v1.476 infrastructure, non-active cycles' pending quests and grind missions live in S.questState.setProgress[setId].pending / .grindPending. So squads assigned to any cycle other than the current active one rendered "free" regardless of whether their characters were actually out on quests. All four call sites now iterate every setProgress[*] entry in addition to the live active-cycle arrays, using the minimum remaining doneAt/endsAt across every source for the time-until-free label. The "click a busy status" ov-char-status modal (v1.6902) was fixed in the same pass: it now finds matches across all cycles, captures the match's setId, and resolves the correct QUESTSETS entry by that id instead of always calling activeQuestSet() — so a non-active-cycle character's modal now shows the correct quest title and set name. Cycle number is omitted when the match isn't the currently-active cycle (non-active setProgress entries don't carry a comparable counter).
Version 1.6904 — Downtime rolls no longer pop an inline dice modal at every completion. (1) runDowntimeCompletion now queues completed downtimes into S.pendingDowntimeRolls and the actual d20 + state mutation is deferred to resolveDowntimeRoll(pending, roll). (2) A new ribbon — "🎲 Downtime completed ×N — tap to roll" — sits between the header and the dev bar and is visible only when the queue is non-empty. Clicking it opens ov-dt-pending, which lists each queued downtime (character · track) and offers a single "🎲 Roll All" button. (3) New runMultiD20Animation draws N independent wireframe d20s in a wider 540×240 arena (ov-dtp-svg); independent positions, velocities, spin drag, and rest-position easing so they settle spread across the floor. Returns an array of N integers; each is piped into resolveDowntimeRoll for its matching queue entry. (4) Every resolved roll appends to S.downtimeRollHistory (capped at 200 entries) with character, track, roll, mods, total, gain, unlock flag, unlock message, and a human-readable timestamp. (5) New Franchise Dashboard tile — 🕯 Downtime Ledger — sits next to Quartermaster with matching chrome (teal gradient instead of navy); shows rolls / unlocks / pending count. Its detail modal mirrors the Quartermaster layout exactly: three-stat bar, dashed-row history list, Cinzel caps dividers. (6) Ribbon re-renders on app load (both cloud and offline paths) so queued rolls from a prior session surface immediately. renderDowntimeRibbon, openDowntimePendingModal, and wiring run once on DOMContentLoaded with _wired guards to avoid double-binding.
Version 1.6903 — Two fixes. (1) Party search now has an ✕ clear button at the right edge of the input that shows only when the field has content. Clicking it empties the input, re-renders the party (restoring the squad panels), and blurs the field. Input now has right-side padding to keep text from colliding with the button. (2) Continue Downtime bulk-resume panel was displaying only the first word of each character's name via name.split(' ')[0] — characters like "The Founder" were rendering as just "The," obscuring whose downtime was being resumed. Both the per-character row labels and the confirmation message now use the full name.
Version 1.6902 — Party screen interactivity pass. (1) Squad roster rows: the status readout on the right side of each row (free / ⚔ X hr / 🕯 X hr) is now a dotted-underline clickable button. For free characters it opens the downtime assignment modal via startDowntimeForChar(ci). For questing characters it opens a new ov-char-status modal showing the active cycle number, quest-set name, quest title, squad, other party members on the mission, and time to return. Grind missions render as "Grind Mission · title" since they aren't in a cycle/set. For downtime characters, the same modal shows the downtime track label (via downtimeTrackLabel), session length, track progress, and time to completion. (2) Removed the "Available" filter button from the party toolbar (kept __not_questing__ tag logic for backward compat in case future filters reuse it). (3) Party search revived: it had been vestigial since char cards were removed in v1.6898. It now scans a rich per-character haystack (name, species, class, background, faith, skills, tools, languages, weapons, inventory, squad names, downtime badges, earned XP badge names, and role flags like hireling/merc/contact/risen/first-hire) and renders the top 5 ranked matches at the top of the Party screen. While the search box is non-empty, the squad panels below are suppressed so only results show. Scoring is multi-token AND (all tokens must match), with a name-match bonus. Each result is a clickable row that opens the character modal.
Version 1.6901 — Five polish fixes. (1) Contracts screen: task collapse arrows (▶/▼) now force text-style rendering via Unicode variation selector \uFE0E. Previously iOS/Safari rendered them as the blue play-emoji; they now match desktop's flat black arrow. (2) Sub-task drag-to-reorder hold time reduced from 500ms to 300ms (standard touch long-press). (3) Add-sub-task field moved to the top of the sub-task list, and newly added sub-tasks now unshift (insert at the top) instead of push (append). Both the "+" button and Enter-key paths updated. The qpScheduleVest tid now references index 0 instead of the old tail index. (4) Party screen: removed the "+ Squad" toolbar button (squad creation still available via ⚔ Manage Squads) and removed the per-squad "Filter by" button from squad panel summaries (filtering is available via the other tag-based filter buttons). (5) Capstone NPC contact rewards: the contact's inventory is now fully marked as personal (ownedItems) on grant, so their gear shows with the 🔒 lock icon and can't be sold or moved to franchise storage. Added a one-time migration at load time that backfills ownedItems for any existing NPC contact whose inventory isn't already personal-flagged.
Version 1.6900 — Five refinements to the character modal. (1) Overview tab tidied: removed redundant Identity block and Squad Membership section (both already shown in the modal header). Replaced with a slim Background + Coin Purse line (gold-tinted purse value). Added a chip row of role/status badges: ⚔ Questing, ⭐ Merc, 🪓 Hireling, 🎖 Risen, 🤝 Contact, 🧭 First Hire. Only chips matching true flags render. Modal header font bumped — title 1.05em → 1.35em, subtitle .72em → .82em. (2) Fixed XP/downtime badge click failure. Root cause: primary delegated click handler was bound to #plist, but the char modal lives outside #plist, so those events never reached it. Added parallel dispatch inside the #ov-charpeek-body scoped listener so XP and downtime badge buttons in the Overview tab open their detail modals (ov-xpbadge, z:510) correctly. (3) Gear tab: renamed "Weapons" header to "Weapon Proficiencies" to reflect that the values are proficiencies, not currently-equipped weapons. (4) Inventory "tap to manage" now opens a proper stacked modal (ov-cpk-inv at z:510) with full-width Sell and Move to Storage buttons, each showing the relevant context inline (e.g. sell value in gp). Replaces the previous inline drawer that appeared below the shared items list. Storage button disabled with tooltip when no building assets are owned. (5) RIF renamed to "Back to HQ" with new semantics: cost 10 QP (was level-scaled GP), the character stays in the party but leaves every squad (was spliced from roster entirely). Auto-disables to "Already at HQ" when the character isn't in any squad. No longer gated by Founder / on-quest / in-downtime — those all allow leaving squad. Graceful fallback alerts when QP is disabled or the balance is insufficient. After success, the char modal's header and Overview refresh so the squad-list line updates immediately.
Version 1.6899 — Restores Level Up and RIF actions that lost their home when char cards were removed in v1.6898. The character modal footer now includes: ⬆ Level Up (left-aligned), ✂ RIF (right-aligned before Close), Close. Both buttons display the cost inline (e.g. "Level Up (125 gp)", "RIF (50 gp)") so you don't have to open a confirmation to see it. RIF auto-disables with explanatory tooltip when: the character is The Founder, is on a quest/grind, or is in downtime. Level Up uses the same ov-lvlup modal with its existing confirmation; after completion, the char modal auto-refreshes its header/body to reflect the new level, skills, tools, etc. RIF uses the same inline confirm flow as before but closes the char modal on success since the character no longer exists. All busy-state and Founder checks still run at click time as belt-and-braces.
Version 1.6898 — Party screen reorganization. (1) Individual character cards removed. All characters now appear inside squad roster panels (from v1.6896). Characters not in any squad appear in a new virtual "Unassigned" panel with a grey border and 🧭 icon, appended after real squads. Virtual = derived each render, not persisted in S.squads — so no lifecycle problems when you rename or rebuild squads. (2) XP / downtime detail modal (ov-xpbadge) z-index elevated to 510 so it layers above the character modal (ov-charpeek, default z:500). Previously the XP modal opened but was hidden behind charpeek due to DOM order. (3) Uninitiated XP tracks (0 XP) no longer each get a full button row in the Overview tab; instead they collapse to a single compact "Not yet started: ⚔ 🗣 🗺 ✨" footer with low-opacity icons. Keeps the focus on tracks the character has actually begun. (4) Gear tab reordered: Weapons → Shared Inventory (clickable) → Pets & Mounts → Personal (at the end since it's display-only). Personal section only renders when the character owns personal items. Known follow-ups: per-character Level Up and RIF buttons lost their home with the card removal — these need new triggers inside the character modal on a later turn. Coin Purse display, ⭐ Merc / 🪓 Hireling / 🎖 Risen / 🤝 Contact / 🧭 First Hire badges, and the inline per-char inventory viewer also no longer appear on the party screen; most are still reachable via the char modal's Overview tab.
Version 1.6897 — Four refinements to the squad roster + character modal from v1.6896. (1) Roster rows now show status icons again: ⚔ prefix on on-quest time, 🕯 prefix on downtime time, nothing for free. Colors also shift (gold for quest, teal for downtime, forest for free). (2) XP and downtime badges in the Overview tab are now clickable buttons using data-a="xp-badge" / data-a="dt-badge" with data-ci — the existing delegated handlers open the same detail modals the party-card pills do. (3) XP and downtime merged into a unified "Earned Badges" section: each XP track renders as a full-width button with icon · label · badge name · XP / next threshold · inline progress bar; downtime badges use the same button pattern with a teal-tinted border and no progress bar (they're discrete achievements, not a progression track). (4) Inventory items are now clickable in the Gear tab. Personal items (c.ownedItems) render as display-only rows with the 🔒 lock icon and "personal" label — no action. Shared items render as clickable buttons; tapping one opens an inline options drawer with 💰 Sell for N gp / 📦 Move to Storage / Cancel. Both actions use the same logic as the party-card sell-inv and to-storage handlers (same gates, same gold math, same franchise-storage lock check). After an action, the drawer closes and the Gear tab re-renders so the updated inventory shows immediately without closing the modal.
Version 1.6896 — Squad roster + character detail overhaul. (1) Roster rows inside collapsible squad panels: dropped species from the meta string, replaced the "⚔ on quest / 🕯 on downtime / free" label with a compact time-until-free readout (e.g. "15 min", "1.25 hr", "2.5d"). Availability computed by scanning questState.pending, grindPending, and downtimeJobs for the earliest doneAt/endsAt whose chars/ci includes the character. (2) Character names in the roster are now clickable — they trigger the existing charpeek delegated handler. (3) ov-charpeek modal fully redesigned with 3 tabs: Overview (identity, squad membership, earned XP per track, downtime badges), Traits (faith, languages, skills, tools), Gear (weapons, inventory, pets & mounts via characterAnimalAssets). Tabs are clickable and swipeable on mobile (50px horizontal threshold, horizontal must dominate vertical by 1.2×, touch-action:pan-y preserves vertical scroll). Tools retained in the modal on the Traits tab since they're conceptually proficiencies.
Version 1.6895 — Contracts screen desktop layout: task button bar was rendering with the 💰 button at half size and all buttons absurdly tall. Two root causes: (1) the inline grid at line 7530 declared repeat(6,minmax(0,1fr)) auto but the actions variable produces 7 buttons (▼▲⇡🕒🗂➕🗂💰) — the 7th got squeezed into the narrow auto column reserved for the sub-task counter. (2) .tacts .btn CSS had aspect-ratio:1/1, so on a wide desktop grid cell (~200px) each button became a 200px square. Fixed by updating the inline grid to repeat(7,minmax(0,1fr)) auto with the counter bumped to grid-column:8, and by removing aspect-ratio:1/1 from the desktop .tacts .btn rule while adding max-height:44px (target was ≤1.5× a sub-task row at 44px). Both duplicated CSS blocks (lines 148 and 1916) updated. Mobile overrides inside @media (max-width:640px) left intact with aspect-ratio:1/1 preserved — square buttons still make sense on narrow screens. Mobile grids also retained at 6 columns; an audit ticket for unifying mobile to 7 columns is a follow-up.
Version 1.6894 — Real fix for class-code (student) login showing "Guest" with empty party and no tutorial. v1.6886's property-rename fix patched the wrong uid(): the file has two function uid() declarations (lines 15684 and 16111 in the prior build). JavaScript hoists both to the top of their scope and the last declaration wins, making the class-code branch dead code that never executed. The var _origUid=window.uid capture at line 15683 even pointed back at the same surviving function, so the intended delegation was a self-reference. Fixed by deleting the dead first declaration and expanding the surviving uid() to handle both identity types: class-code sessions check currentUser._codeLogin and return currentUser.player_user_id first; regular sessions return currentUser.user.id as before. Also updated currentUserEmail() to return "Code: XXXXXX" for code-login users instead of empty string, so the Settings screen stops reading "Guest" after a successful login. Once uid() returns non-null for code sessions, sbPull's existing code-session branch (line 16783) runs normally: fetches player_data from login_codes, either restores saved state or hits the fresh-account path that calls applyStarterLoadout(true) — which is what produces the welcome modal, tutorial task, and The Founder character.
Version 1.6893 — Fix: when creating a squad via the expensive "+500 gp includes Bag of Holding" path, the bag would sometimes land in the franchise Assets & Investments panel instead of being bound to the new squad. Root cause: the asset pushed at line 10480 carried no direct bagOfHolding / effect.squadFormation flags; it relied on a GEAR-list round-trip via hasSquadFormationEffect. If a user's DB gear row for "Bag of Holding" (saved through Quest Editor) lacked those flags, applyDBItemsToArrays at line 19298 fully replaced the built-in entry — wiping them — and the check returned false, so sqSave's filter excluded the asset and the bag stayed unassigned in partyAssets. Two-layered fix: (1) set bagOfHolding:true and effect:{squadFormation:true} directly on the asset at creation so recognition doesn't depend on a GEAR lookup; (2) hasSquadFormationEffect now falls back to BUILTIN_GEAR when the merged GEAR entry is missing flags, catching other code paths (franchiseStorage migration, recovery, future entrypoints) exposed to the same stripping.
Version 1.6892 — Party screen: collapsible squad panels now show a thin-row roster of members when expanded. Each row shows name, species · class · Lv, and current status (⚔ on quest / 🕯 on downtime / free). Sorted by level desc, class asc, name asc. Empty squads show "No members." The existing assets and role-kit rows follow below. Reuses the questingChars and downtimeChars maps already computed at the top of renderParty, so no additional state scans.
Version 1.6891 — Prereq vocabulary audit: added Vehicle (Land) and Vehicle (Water) to DND.tools. These values were already offered by the Quest Editor as tool prereqs but the main app's character generator never produced them, making any quest with those prereqs impossible to satisfy. Adding them to the main app's universe means they now appear (1) in the random recruiter roll at a 1-in-28 chance per tool slot, (2) in the premium hand-pick recruiter's tool pills, (3) in the recruitment wish-list ribbon, (4) in the squad manager tool ribbon, (5) as valid prereq satisfiers in questPrereqCheck. Quest Editor updated to v1.673 alongside this change to add Healer's Kit which the main app was producing but the editor could not require.
Version 1.6890 — Countdown readability at multi-day durations. (1) Back Burner task deferrals now display as "Xd Yh Zm" when 48h or more remain (was "72 hr 15 min", now "3d 0 hr 15 min"). Under 48h keeps the existing hr / hr+min format so short deferrals read the same. (2) The shared fmtCountdown helper used by quest, grind, and downtime tickers now also breaks out days at 48h+, dropping the seconds field at that range (ticking seconds on a multi-day countdown adds no value and flickers the UI).
Version 1.6889 — Fix: Task Edit modal was wiping subtask completion state. The Save handler at line 7996 did t.subtasks=subLines.map(n=>({name:n,done:false})), hardcoding every subtask back to undone on every save — even if only the title or gold value changed. Fixed by preserving existing done flags via a name-indexed lookup (with duplicate-name positional matching), so renamed or added lines get done:false but untouched or reordered lines keep their state. Also added a proactive t.pct recompute so the progress pie reflects reality immediately if the user deleted a done subtask.
Version 1.6888 — QP Manager: split Short Rest and Early Completion into separate cost fields. Previously both shared costShortRest, making it impossible to tune them independently despite very different mechanics (Short Rest pre-pays a fraction and the quest continues; Early Completion skips the timer and pays the full remainder). New field costEarlyCompletion (default 10 QP); existing costShortRest unchanged (default 5 QP). Labels clarified: "Short rest (pre-earn gp, quest continues)" and "Early completion (skip timer, full reward)". Handler at line 13680 now branches on _isEarly. Button rendering also updated so each button shows its actual cost; also added a missing QP-balance gate to the Early Complete button (previously it rendered regardless of balance).
Version 1.6887 — Fix: a character on a quest/grind could sometimes be sent on downtime, bypassing gates. Root cause: startDowntimeForChar (line 8716) checks questBusyForChar when the modal opens, but confirmDowntimeModal had no matching recheck when the modal's Confirm button was clicked. Between open and confirm, the character could be sent on a quest in a separate flow (two tabs, stale render, long-lived modal) and the confirm would push an overlapping downtime job. Added defense-in-depth rechecks in confirmDowntimeModal: both questBusyForChar(ci) and downtimeJobForChar(ci) now run at confirm time, with alerts and modal-close fallbacks. The reverse direction (downtime → quest) already has a forfeit-modal handler at line 14452.
Version 1.6886 — Fix: class-code (student) first-login was broken. At line 15551 the currentUser object set user_id:row.player_user_id, but the patched uid() function reads currentUser.player_user_id. On first login this check failed and uid() fell through to returning _codeId — a different UUID than what any Supabase data was keyed under. Result: new students saw empty party, no welcome modal, no tutorial task. On reload the session-restore path (line 15672) set the property correctly so subsequent loads behaved differently again. Fixed by renaming the property to player_user_id so uid() returns the correct stable UUID on every login path.
Version 1.6885 — Post-Quest Resolution plumbing (Phase 1 of spec). Added 6 new content_types to CONTENT_LIB / CONTENT_TYPE_MAP / CONTENT_LIST_MAP / loadContentLibrary routing: monster, trait, terrain, condition, game_rules, outcome_template. No player-visible changes yet; new buckets populate from Supabase on load but are unconsumed. Supabase constraint expanded and default vocabulary seeded in Parts A and B migrations.
Version 1.6883 — Downtime duration now has mechanical weight. 8h is the standard baseline (no modifier). 1h: −3, 4h: −1, 12h: +1, 24h: +3 applied to the d20 roll before comparing against the gain thresholds (6/11/16). The datetime-local picker is removed — pills are now the only selection method. Default selection is 8h. The roll result modal shows stat modifier and duration modifier as separate line items. Both the main downtime modal and the "Continue Downtime" QP picker use the updated 1/4/8/12/24h pill set.
Version 1.6882 — Fix: squadReqMax prerequisite row showed as not-met when one character filled multiple slots. The check was counting total slot assignments instead of unique characters — if Lindir filled all 4 slots, _assigned was 4, making 4 <= 1 false. Fixed by deduplicating the char index list before comparing against squadReqMax.
Version 1.6881 — Fix: abandoning a quest cycle now clears grind missions from both the live questState.grindPending and the saved setProgress[setId].grindPending snapshot. Previously, active grind missions with their old squad char indices survived the abandon and were restored when re-entering the same cycle with a new squad — causing phantom busy characters and wrong names showing as in-progress grinders. Also added a safety filter in loadSetProgress that drops any already-completed grind missions (doneAt in the past) when loading a cycle.
Version 1.6880 — Auto-Assign Grind button now reads QP cost from settings (qpCfg().costGrindAuto) instead of hardcoding 50. Both button labels and the _canAutoGrind balance check update when the QP Manager cost is changed.
Version 1.6879 — Critical fix: projects were not persisting to Supabase. Three bugs: (1) Race condition — regular active_tasks and project rows were pushed as two separate ops in Promise.all(), both starting with DELETE active_tasks. The regular delete was wiping project rows that the project op had just re-inserted, or vice versa. Fixed by merging both into a single delete+insert op. (2) Empty projects were silently dropped — if a project had no tasks, projRows was empty and the insert was skipped entirely, losing the project on next pull. Fixed by writing a sentinel row (name='__proj_meta__') for every project regardless of task count. (3) Pull skipped S.projects assignment when _projTasks was empty, leaving projects=[]. Fixed by always running the rebuild and reading sentinel rows to restore empty projects.
Version 1.6878 — Squad manager desktop: (1) Member count and coverage % now appear as a second line inside each squad tile (below the name), on both regular and Unassigned tiles. (2) Transfer buttons now show a green +X% coverage delta when moving that character into the destination squad would improve its coverage. Characters that add no new coverage show 0%.
Version 1.6877 — Squad manager desktop: (1) Transfer column widened from 54px to 80px; buttons widened from 44px to 72px; button labels now show first name on line 1, surname on line 2, arrow on line 3 — no mid-word breaks. (2) Unassigned character cards no longer inherit the first squad's color; the virtual squad's _virtual flag is now checked before calling squadColor(), defaulting to the neutral parchment background.
Version 1.6876 — Squad manager desktop: (1) Coverage % shown on every squad tile including Unassigned (format: "N · X%"). (2) Unassigned now works as a proper transfer column — clicking its tile loads all unassigned characters into the left or right column, with transfer buttons that move characters to/from it like any other squad.
Version 1.6875 — Squad manager desktop: grid changed from 4×2 to 8×1 layout so all squad tiles fit in a single compact row. Tiles switch to a horizontal chip style (emoji + name side by side) with no aspect-ratio constraint, giving the two squad columns below maximum height.
Version 1.6874 — Squad manager desktop improvements: (1) Squad grid tiles shrink significantly on ≥700px screens (smaller font, hidden member-count row, tighter gaps) so the two squad columns get maximum vertical space. (2) Desktop transfer buttons now enforce all four rules: (a) territory — both locked squads must share the same territory or both have none; (b) busy — character cannot be on an active quest, grind, or downtime job; (c) max size — dest squad cannot exceed 10 members; (d) if dest is full, the existing swap suggestion modal opens automatically so the user can pick an optimal swap candidate.
Version 1.6873 — Squad manager desktop layout: on screens ≥700px the modal widens to 82vw and the swap zone is replaced by a 3-column layout — left squad | ⇄ Transfer column | right squad. Tap any two squad tiles in the grid to load them into the columns (tiles show L/R badges). The transfer column shows a button per character in each squad; clicking it moves that character across and spends the configured QP cost. Character cards in the desktop columns show all skills, languages, and faith by default. Mobile layout is unchanged.
Version 1.6872 — Continue Downtime panel (party screen) is now collapsible. Expanded view shows each eligible character with a checkbox (all checked by default), their best active track name, and a mini progress bar (e.g. 3 / 8). The ⚡ QP button updates live as checkboxes are toggled — cost reflects only the selected characters. Unchecking all disables the button. The duration picker that follows only queues jobs for the checked subset.
Version 1.6871 — Fix: 💰 Collect Gold button now matches the size of the other action row buttons on mobile. The earlier .tacts .btn.bg font-size fix was being overridden by the mobile media query rules (@media max-width:600px and max-width:420px), which set .tacts .btn font-size after the fix rule in the cascade. Added matching .tacts .btn.bg overrides inside both breakpoints in both style blocks.
Version 1.6870 — Sub-task drag handle hold delay increased from 320ms to 500ms and movement cancel threshold from 8px to 14px, making accidental drags much less likely on mobile. The handle now scales up during the arming window as a clearer visual cue that drag is about to activate.
Version 1.6869 — Downtime merge threshold fix: when duplicate progress tracks are merged (either automatically via sanitizeDowntimeKeys on load, or manually via the ⇄ Merge button), the combined value is now checked against the completion threshold. If it meets or exceeds the threshold, the full reward is granted immediately — skill learned, badge awarded, log entry written — and the remainder is kept. An alert surfaces the unlock message so it isn't silent.
Version 1.6868 — Downtime modal: a “↩ Resume last track” pill now appears at the top when the character has prior progress. Shows the track name and current progress bar (e.g. 3 / 8). Tapping it auto-selects the matching activity and target in the dropdowns so the user can confirm without hunting through the lists.
Version 1.6867 — Downtime duplicate progress: the Night Owl badge modal now detects when a character has split progress bars for the same track (caused by the now-fixed prefix-stripping bug). Duplicate tracks are labelled, their values shown combined, and a ⇄ Merge button appears inline. Tapping Merge folds all dirty keys into the canonical clean key, saves, and re-renders the modal body without closing — no page reload needed.
Version 1.6866 — Downtime bug fix: the 🔄 prefix was not being stripped from target values before storing them as dtProgress keys. Root cause was a broken regex using a character class [★🔄] — because 🔄 is a surrogate pair (U+1F504), the two halves were treated as separate characters and never matched the full emoji. Changed to a proper alternation (?:★|🔄) in all three stripping sites. Added a standalone sanitizeDowntimeKeys() that (1) merges any polluted dtProgress keys into their clean equivalents and (2) strips the prefix off any in-flight downtimeJob targets. Called at all three load paths (local load, code login, and main Supabase pull) so corrupted save data heals immediately on next session open.
Version 1.6865 — Two improvements: (1) Sub-task move: each sub-task row now has a ⇄ button that opens a modal to move the sub-task to any other active task. The destination task auto-expands so the moved item is immediately visible. Pie progress recalculates on both source and destination tasks. (2) Collect gold (💰) button in the task action row is now the same visual size as the surrounding icon buttons — targeted font-size rule for .tacts .btn.bg prevents the emoji from rendering oversized.
Version 1.6864 — Quest modal peg slot improvements: (1) When a quest has squadReqMax set, support slots are no longer padded beyond that number — you cannot assign more characters than the max allows. Required prereq slots always appear regardless of the max. (2) Auto-scroll carousel now fires correctly on any page — when all visible slots on the current page are filled, the carousel advances to the next page with the existing springy animation. Previously the condition required all 6 slots to be non-support prereqs, which almost never fired.
Version 1.6863 — Fix pets and custom items never loading in store. Two fixes: (1) bootApp() now calls refreshSession(true) before initSession() so expired JWTs are refreshed at startup; if refresh fails the session is cleared and re-login is prompted. (2) The initSession .catch block now also calls loadPetsFromDB()+loadItemsFromDB()+renderStore() so store content always loads even when sbPull fails. Root cause: stored JWT was expired, sbPull 401'd, promise chain rejected, loadPetsFromDB never called.
Version 1.6861 — Fix custom items (e.g. Boxing Gear) and pets not showing in store. Four bugs fixed: (1) loadPetsFromDB query now filters published=eq.true so unpublished pets are excluded and RLS is satisfied. (2) renderStore() added to the sbPull completion chain so it fires after loadContentLibrary + loadPetsFromDB + loadItemsFromDB all resolve. (3) Guest/offline path now uses Promise.all([loadPetsFromDB, loadItemsFromDB]).then(renderStore) instead of fire-and-forget. (4) loadContentLibrary's internal renderStore() is now guarded — only fires if DB_GEAR/PETS caches are already populated, preventing a race where mergeCustomContent resets GEAR to built-ins before loadItemsFromDB has returned.
Version 1.6860 — QP Manager access gate removed in -in-development build — any logged-in account can now open it. Previously restricted to app admins and test accounts.
Version 1.6859 — Developer Tools section restored to Settings for all logged-in users. "Enable Dev Mode" button sets devModeOverride=true and reveals all dev sections (quest test tools, content editor, milestone loader, d20 table, data tools). "Exit Dev Mode" button in settings and dev bar both disable it. Previously dev mode was only activatable via ?dev=1 URL param or admin account.
Version 1.6858 — Hotfix: missing comma after qpLastLoginDate in createDefaultState() caused SyntaxError on load, breaking all script execution (signIn not defined). Added trailing comma.
Version 1.6857 — Three new QP income streams: (1) Daily Login Loyalty — awards base 10 QP on first login of each day; consecutive days increase by 5 QP/day up to 50 QP cap; missing a day drops by 5 (floors at base). (2) Grind completion — awards 2 QP per grind collected. (3) Quest completion — 5 QP per regular quest, 20 QP per capstone (first completion only, idempotency-keyed). All three rates plus loyalty base/step/cap are editable in the QP Manager earn section. Loyalty streak and last-login date shown in manager for diagnostics.
Version 1.6856 — Task title is now the collapse/expand toggle (shows ▶/▼ prefix when subtasks exist). Edit contract moved to a ? icon button on the right of the title row. colBtn standalone button removed.
Version 1.6855 — Sub-task reordering upgraded from ▲/▼ buttons to drag-and-drop handles (⁞ grip icon). Mouse drag uses HTML5 DnD; touch drag uses a ghost-clone follow with drop-on-release. Both modes constrain reorder within the parent task and call save() on drop. Dead stup/stdn button handlers removed.
Version 1.6854 — Quest order fix: content_items query now fetches with order=sort_order.asc so quests arrive from DB in the correct sequence, matching the quest editor.
Version 1.6853 — Quest order root fix: custom-only cycles (not in built-in sets) now receive _questOrder when first pushed into QUESTSETS in mergeCustomContent. Previously the else branch omitted it, so questOrder from the editor was silently discarded.
Version 1.6852 — Quest cycle abandon fix: abandoning a cycle now clears the active cycle without auto-reassigning to another. User chooses their next cycle manually via Quest Map.
Version 1.6851 — Removed ▲/▼ reorder buttons from quest editor left panel. Quest order is now managed exclusively via the Quest Sequence Order panel inside the cycle form.
Version 1.6850 — Quest order root fix: left-panel ▲/▼ reorder now swaps sort_order (not idx), normalises positions to 0,1,2..., and saves questOrder to set metadata in the same operation. Both sort systems now agree.
Version 1.6849 — Quest order fixes: (1) Dupe cycle form initialises from _questOrder not raw idx. (2) Dupe save stores questOrder on new set metadata. (3) Cycle rules save preserves existing questOrder. (4) Editor left panel sorts by sort_order to match game.
Version 1.6848 — Quest cycle fixes: (1) Abandon button now always shows on the active cycle regardless of squad assignment — prevents getting stuck after early completion. (2) Early completion now correctly awards capstone reward and logs to questLog.
Version 1.6847 — Early completion fix: now correctly awards capstone reward, logs to questLog, and sets repeatCounts so cycle-complete state is properly detected.
Version 1.6846 — Quest order fix: quests now sorted by sort_order (editor position) instead of internal idx number, in both loadContentLibrary and mergeCustomContent.
Version 1.6845 — Always land on Contracts screen after load — navTo('tasks') called explicitly after sbPull completes in all paths (regular, code-session, offline catch).
Version 1.6844 — Desktop layout: all fixed elements and app content forced to full width with !important overrides.
Version 1.6843 — Desktop layout fix: header and store footer now pin left:0 and span full viewport width, matching the bottom nav.
Version 1.6842 — Desktop layout: header, store footer, and app content now expand to full viewport width on screens 600px and wider.
Version 1.6841 — Code sessions now use login_codes.player_data blob for save/load, completely bypassing auth-gated tables. New code accounts get applyStarterLoadout(). Requires SQL: ALTER TABLE login_codes ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS player_data jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb; and RLS: CREATE POLICY "Code holders can update their own slot" ON login_codes FOR UPDATE USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
Version 1.684 — Class Codes: teachers can generate short alphanumeric codes (e.g. X7K2-M9QP) for students. No email or PII required. Code tab on login screen, code generator in Settings, bulk generate 30 codes, copy-to-clipboard export. Sessions persist across browser reloads.
Version 1.684 — Class code login: teacher generates X7K2-style codes from Settings. Students enter code on Class Code tab — no email or PII required. Each code maps to an independent save slot. Bulk generate 30 codes, copy-to-clipboard export. Requires login_codes table in Supabase (see SQL below).
Version 1.683 — Projects: accessed via button on Contracts screen (like Archive, not in bottom nav). Task transfer (⇄) moves tasks between projects preserving pct/subtask progress. Pinterest masonry grid with Pinterest-style masonry card grid. Each project has tasks with subtasks. Tasks earn gold to the shared pool. Promote any contract to a project with one button. Projects persist via active_tasks table using cat=__project__ sentinel.
Version 1.682 — Tutorial task seeded in applyStarterLoadout() so it exists in S.active before the very first renderTasks() call — visible immediately on first load without any tab switching.
Version 1.68111 — Tasks load immediately: renderTasks() now fires as the very first render call in initSession (right after load() restores S.active from localStorage), and again first in the sbPull.then chain.
Version 1.6811 — Contracts load fix: renderTasks() now also called in sbPull catch block (offline/slow network path) and deferred 150ms after initial render to catch settled state.
Version 1.681 — (1) Task cards show ℹ button when desc is set; opens info modal. (2) Tutorial task with 8 subtasks seeded on first welcome dismiss. (3) Task desc stored/restored via __meta in Supabase.
Version 1.680 — Dev mode cycle completion: (1) Jump to Cycle 2 now marks all quests of the outgoing cycle complete in both questCycles archive and setProgress, so isSetCompleted() correctly unlocks dependent cycles. (2) +1 Day also pushes quest idx into done[] and repeatCounts so jump picks them up correctly.
Version 1.679 — Capstone reward dashboard cards: (1) awardCapstonReward now calls _autoCreateDashCard when an override reward is granted. (2) getDashboardCards auto-generates missing dc_cap_ cards for any already-completed capstone quests that lack a card.
Version 1.6781 — Fix: grind enc-card data-qi injection used q.idx/q.setId but the grind loop variable is g — causing ReferenceError on renderAdv.
Version 1.678 — (1) Party screen: sticky search box filters by name/skill/tool/lang/inv/weapon. (2) Level-up in quest modal refreshes peg slot cards immediately. (3) Post-purchase: already-placed characters auto-assigned to newly-satisfied prereq slots.
Version 1.677 — Purchase button fixes: (1) Char peg slots (item prereqs like Longsword) now show a 🛒 Purchase button when unmet. (2) Purchase button in qcs-info-prereqs moved outside the overflow:hidden span so it is no longer clipped/unclickable.
Version 1.676 — Building purchase fix: ownerVal was "__storage__" but handler checked "storage" (no underscores) — buildings fell through all delivery conditions and were silently dropped. Added __storage__ to isStructuredOwner check and delivery path. Buildings now correctly land in partyAssets.
Version 1.675 — Store bugs fixed: (1) Search wrong item — Buy button now uses data-name instead of data-i; purchase confirmation resolves by name across all arrays, safe against filtered results. (2) qcs-store-link button style cleaned up (removed row-layout artifacts).
Version 1.674 — Dev mode: ?dev=1 activates without email restriction. devStampUids() hooked into renderParty and renderAdv. Store cards show loop index + _dbId. Both asset paths show uid + _dbId.
Version 1.673 — Dev mode UID overlay: character cards show ci+_dbId, asset rows show uid+_dbId, squad tiles show sq.id, quest cards show idx+setId, cycle headings show set.id. All badges hidden outside dev mode.
Version 1.672 — Squad formation fixes: (1) Capstone item reward now copies effect+bagOfHolding onto partyAsset so hasSquadFormationEffect can detect it. (2) Random-pool bag rewards go to partyAssets (not franchiseStorage) with effect flag. (3) sqSave migrates any legacy franchiseStorage squad-formation items to partyAssets before searching. (4) franchiseHasBagOfHolding checks both partyAssets and legacy franchiseStorage.
Version 1.671 — (1) Welcome Banquet 5-min override removed; first-quest 3-min rule now applies. (2) Franchise dashboard closes on any nav press. (3) Prereq store button moved to its own row beneath the slot value. (4) Building purchases from prereq modal restricted to Franchise assignment only.
Version 1.670 — All fuzzy bag-name matching removed. _isBagOfHolding and franchiseHasBagOfHolding now delegate entirely to hasSquadFormationEffect. Squad formation is determined by effect flag only.
Version 1.669 — Squad formation redesigned: new squad_formation effect type added. hasSquadFormationEffect() replaces fuzzy _isBagOfHolding() matching in sqSave. Built-in Bag of Holding and Longinus Carpetbag get effect:{squadFormation:true}. sqSave only searches unassigned partyAssets — no storage/inventory fallbacks.
Version 1.668 — Quest order now persists: loadContentLibrary copies row.sort_order onto item objects; mergeCustomContent sorts by sort_order then idx.
Version 1.667 — Quest editor: Max Party Size field added (squadReqMax). Enforced in questHardGateIssues (blocks Begin if over cap). Shown in prereq info card. Saved in both quest editors.
Version 1.666 — Quest editor: position dropdown replaces ▲/▼ arrows for reordering; NPC contact editor adds level field; main app uses override.level on NPC join.
Version 1.665 — NPC contact editor: class dropdown auto-rolls stats per CLASS_STATS, re-roll button, quest limit field (questsRemaining). Main app decrements questsRemaining on quest collect and removes NPC when it reaches 0.
Version 1.664 — Rope of Climbing description revised.
Version 1.663 — Rising Reputation capstone now has capstoneReward: Rope of Climbing.
Version 1.662 — Dev-only: ⬡ Flow tab in quest editor renders ASCII flowchart of quest cycle chains, tier groupings, capstone labels, and cycle-back arrows. Copy button included.
Version 1.661 — Continue Downtime bar now excludes characters who are currently on an active quest (questState.pending with doneAt in the future).
Version 1.660 — Fix: qholeCarApply dynamic height used allSlots (out of scope); corrected to slots.length.
Version 1.659 — buildingReq now generates a nonChar peg slot in the quest assignment carousel (met automatically when the franchise owns the building in partyAssets). Previously it only appeared in the info card as a bonus-reward row.
Version 1.658 — Level prereq peg slots no longer show the hireling-hire prompt. Hirelings cannot satisfy a level requirement.
Version 1.657 — Peg slot carousel: viewport height and grid-template-rows now set dynamically based on actual row count (92px/row). 6-slot quests (3 rows) get 290px viewport; 4-slot quests (2 rows) get ~200px. No more clipped bottom slots.
Version 1.656 — Tutorial checklist rolled back. Welcome modal restored.
Version 1.655 — Tutorial: setOpen now uses direct style.visibility + style.transform instead of classList — eliminates CSS specificity race. Panel primed offscreen before animation on welcome-ok.
Version 1.654 — Tutorial: TUT.load() now runs before welcome modal so _active is set correctly; panel uses visibility+transform instead of display:none so classList changes work reliably; welcome-ok delays sheet open 120ms so modal closes first.
Version 1.653 — Tutorial redesigned as full slide-up sheet (66vh): dim overlay, drag handle, scrollable body, floating tab when minimised.
Version 1.652 — Tutorial panel: fixed positioning above nav with safe-area-inset, max-width:480px centered to match nav, z-index:9000, pointer-events:all on container (no more invisible overlay blocking clicks).
Version 1.651 — Tutorial: welcome modal replaced with ranger intro; TUT init moved to initSession with correct uid; welcome OK shows checklist; Skip dismisses tutorial; revive opens immediately.
Version 1.650 — Tutorial/onboarding checklist: 8-step legal-pad panel, gruff ranger narrator, QP rewards, auto-detection of completion, seeded hirelings for tutorial steps 5 and 8, skip/revive via settings.
Version 1.649 — (19) Hireling reroll count tracked in hir-box reroll button too. (20) First quest duration reduced to 3 minutes. (21) Quest nav button scrolls to top of screen instead of next card.
Version 1.648 — (16) h1 title: leading ⚔ removed, trailing kept. (17) btn-randall hidden during first recruit session. (18) After 3 hireling rerolls: modal tips player about in-quest hire and waives the 75 gp surcharge on their next use.
Version 1.647 — (10) Reroll button hidden on free first recruit. (11) Inactive recruit buttons minimize when one is selected. (12) Party nav icon reverted to ⚔. (13) No-squad modal suppressed until first grind complete. (14) Quest button scrolls to top of screen, not next card. (15) h1 title emoji cleaned.
Version 1.646 — (6) Recruit chooser buttons: title stacked above description. (7) Squad intro suppressed until first quest cycle complete. (8) Adventurer panel open now injects starter guide candidate. (9) Wishlist hidden during first recruit action.
Version 1.645 — Hireling hire fix: _recruitInProgress now set to true when adventurer or hireling panel opens, keeping the recruit screen live during the hire flow.
Version 1.644 — (1) Party nav icon changed from ⚔ to 👥. (2) Hireling panel auto-roll fixed: no longer calls .click() which triggered navTo(party) and navigated away before hiring was possible.
Version 1.643 — (1) First cycle launch no longer triggers squad intro when no squads exist. (2) Recruit screen consolidated: 3 chooser buttons reveal panels inline. (3) Login pills reset to 38-48.
Version 1.642 — Pet prereq architecture fix: pet slots are now char slots (nonChar=false), exactly like skill/tool slots. The qualifying character is the one who owns the matching pet. Only asset/bag prereqs remain as nonChar tiles. qddCharCoversSlot already returns true for the pet owner.
Version 1.641 — Pet prereq fixes: (1) Purchase button suppressed for pet slots (pets aren't in the store). (2) qddCharCoversSlot now lights up the pet's owner char as a placement candidate. (3) Unowned/squad pets still allow any char to satisfy the slot.
Version 1.640 — Prereq slot classification fix: Bag of Holding now classified as an asset at slot-build time (nonChar=true, isAsset=true) so it renders as a prereq tile rather than a char slot. Any pet name (including char-owned party pets) classified as isPet=true at slot-build time. Pet prereq tile turns green when the owning char is placed in a peg slot.
Version 1.639 — Prereq precision fixes: (1) Bag/squad assets satisfy the nonChar prereq SLOT only — no longer marks individual char peg-cards. (2) Char-owned pets require that char to be placed in a peg slot; unowned/squad pets count freely. Gear prereqs restored to char-inv only.
Version 1.638 — Prereq fixes: (1) Bag of Holding now recognized as an asset prereq (routes to partyAssets+storage, not char inv). (2) Pet prereqs check all party pets, not just peg-slot char's pet. (3) Gear prereqs also check squad partyAssets so shared party items count. (4) isPetItemName checks partyAssets entries for char-owned pets. (5) qddCharCoversSlot checks squad assets so dragging any char satisfies asset/bag prereq slots.
Version 1.637 — Three bag-of-holding fixes: (1) sqSave now searches all bags not just unowned ones. (2) Squad deletion uses _isBagOfHolding() instead of exact name "Bag of Holding" so Longinus variants are returned to storage. (3) On load, bag ownerRefs pointing to renamed/deleted squads are cleared so franchiseHasBagOfHolding can find them.
Version 1.636 — Bug fix: franchiseHasBagOfHolding() was excluding squad-assigned bags (ownerType==='squad'). Both franchise bags are assigned to squads so the 50 gp enrollment route was never unlocking. Squad-owned bags now count.
Version 1.635 — Bag of Holding recovery: after sbPull, if franchiseStorage contains a bag-of-holding name but partyAssets is missing it (lost in a failed push), the bag is automatically restored to partyAssets. sbPush party_assets insert failures now log a visible warning instead of silently losing data.
Version 1.634 — Quest Map Collect All now sweeps both completed regular quests and completed grind missions. Grind collection uses the full gcollect flow: 90/10 split, char coin purses, item purchases, quest log entry, and immediate sbPush.
Version 1.633 — Root & Branch capstone reward set to "Longinus Carpetbag of Holding" (bagOfHolding:true so it counts for the 50 gp squad enrollment route).
Version 1.632 — Squad swap: characters currently on an active quest, grind, or downtime job are now excluded from all swap candidate pools (symmetric squad↔squad, asymmetric unassigned↔squad, and the unassigned virtual squad). Added _buildBusySet() helper.
Version 1.631 — New QP features: (a) Quest Map Collect All fast-forwards all naturally-completed quests silently with a summary. (b) QP-to-GP icon above store search with 10/25/50/75/100%% pill options; also on unmet gold prereq peg-slots in quest assignment. (c) Short Rest and Early Completion on in-progress quest cards: auto-offered based on elapsed fraction (1/3, 1/2, 3/4+), temporary payout continues quest, early completion ends it. (d) startedAt timestamp on quest pending entries. All costs/rates configurable in QP Manager.
Version 1.630 — Carousel arrows now overlap viewport by half their width: hole carousel margin 32px→14px, char carousel margin 30px→13px.
Version 1.629 — Quest assignment peg slots: changed from 1x4 to 2x3 grid (2 columns, 3 rows per page). Viewport adjusted to 260px.
Version 1.628 — quest_prereqs DB table support: loadContentLibrary now fetches structured prereqs and attaches as q._prereqs[]. questPrereqCheck and qddBuildInfoPrereqs use _prereqs when present, falling back to q.pre blob for older quests. Editor saves/loads prereqs to quest_prereqs table.
Version 1.627 — Fix peg slot viewport: qhole-car-track and qhole-car-page now have height:100% so repeat(4,1fr) rows divide the viewport correctly. Removed min-height:64px floor that was causing overflow. Removed translateY carousel offset that caused mobile overlap.
Version 1.626 — QP manager: added configurable costs for (a) cancel/recall quest (costRecall, default 25), (b) advance downtime per job (costDowntime, default 5), (c) grind auto-assign (costGrindAuto, default 50). All three were previously hardcoded.
Version 1.625 — (1) Peg slots: 1x4 per page, second page has 4 optional slots. (2) Party carousel lifted up by half a slot height.
Version 1.624 — Fix: qddCharCoversSlot now checks actual c.inv for item slots instead of returning true for any char. Hirelings and chars without the required item in inventory will no longer show a match tag or be accepted into an item peg slot. Removed redundant itemTags block in qddRenderPeg — matchedSlots now drives all tags consistently.
Version 1.623 — Fix: Personal items (🔒) section now self-heals — if an item is in c.ownedItems but missing from c.inv (e.g. after a migration or DB round-trip), it is re-added to inv so it appears in the card. migrateMisroutedAssets now also preserves ownedItems tagging when moving character-personal assets from partyAssets to c.inv.
Version 1.622 — (1) Peg slot viewport expanded to 360px showing 5 slots per page. (2) Collapsible header panel: replaced animated card carousel with a flat 1x3 column of panels (Description, Details, Best Fit) that stretch to full content height when expanded.
Version 1.621 — (1) Gear item peg slots now accept character placement — qddCharCoversSlot returns true for item type, and only pet/asset items remain nonChar status tiles. (2) Party carousel hugs the button row via margin-top:auto in a flex-column scroll container.
Version 1.620 — (1) Item prereq slot now only shows met when a char carrying the item is placed in a peg slot — not just any squad/party member. (2) Peg slots reduced to 1x4 with second page right. (3) Quest modal scroll locked vertically unless accessibility font is set above Normal (18px).
Version 1.619 — Quest modal: (1) Fixed collapsible title panel — removed overflow:hidden which was clipping the absolute overlay. (2) Restored 1x6 peg slot grid. (3) Modal expanded to 96vh to use nearly full screen height.
Version 1.618 — Quest modal fine-tuning: (1) Expanded title panel overlaps lower content instead of pushing it down. (2) Level prereq is now a real drop-target peg slot accepting chars of sufficient level. (3) Unmet item prereq slots show a Purchase button linking to the store. (4) Peg slot grid changed to 1x5.
Version 1.617 — Quest modal redesign: (1) Title/cards collapsed into a closed details bar by default. (2) Level, gold, item, pet, mount prereqs now appear as nonchar status tiles in peg slot area with live checkmarks. (3) Peg slots: 1 column x 6 rows, extra slots page right. (4) Body font sizes increased. (5) Button row hugs bottom.
Version 1.616 — Comprehensive prereq check rewrite. questPrereqCheck and questPrereqHtml now use consistent logic: skills/tools/lang/faith from all peg-slot chars; item gear/weapon from non-busy peg-slot c.inv only; vehicle/building items from partyAssets+storage; pet items from pet assets owned by non-busy squad members. Level prereq now excludes busy chars. Added isAssetItemName() and isPetItemName() helpers for routing.
Version 1.615 — Fix: 🎒 Longsword contribution showing for chars outside the active squad. buildQuestContributionHtml now checks whether the char is a member of the active cycle squad before showing item contributions. If no squad is assigned to the cycle, all chars are eligible as before.
Version 1.614 — (1) Level-up in quest modal now calls updateQuestModal() after re-rendering so the prereq check and Begin button refresh immediately. (2) buildQuestContributionHtml [indicator a] now respects isBusy for item contributions — busy chars no longer show 🎒 item match. (3) Carousel item tags [indicator c] now filters busy chars so item match tags only show for available chars. All three prereq indicators now agree.
Version 1.613 — Auto-migrate misrouted gear assets: weapons/armor/gear that ended up in partyAssets (due to old party:true bug on all DB items) are automatically moved to the correct character's c.inv on pull. Unowned gear assets move to franchise storage. Runs once after sbPull completes.
Version 1.612 — Fix prereq: busy chars (on another quest) can no longer satisfy item prerequisites. questPrereqCheck now builds a busy set from questState.pending and excludes busy char inventories from the item inv pool. Also fixed qddSyncCheckboxes to never check busy chars in peg slots. Skills/tools/lang prereqs still count all assigned chars since proficiencies exist regardless of current quest assignment.
Version 1.611 — Fix 3 bugs: (1) Prereq check counting busy chars: getQuestChars now also checks questState.pending to exclude busy chars even if their checkbox is still enabled. (2) Sell price 1gp: mergeCustomContent was resetting GEAR to built-ins, wiping DB items like Longsword. DB items now stored in DB_GEAR/DB_VEHICLES/DB_BUILDINGS caches and re-applied after every mergeCustomContent via applyDBItemsToArrays(). (3) Item not leaving inventory: same root cause as (2) — GEAR lookup failed so item type routing broke. Fixed by same applyDBItemsToArrays mechanism.
Version 1.610 — Fix: quest prereq check incorrectly passing when a busy character was previously assigned to the quest. Disabled+checked checkboxes were still read by querySelectorAll. Fixed in two places: getQuestChars now uses :not(:disabled) selector, and _prevAssigned filters out busy characters so they are never pre-checked.
Version 1.609 — Fix: weapons, armor, and gear purchased from store were routing to party assets panel instead of character inventory. loadItemsFromDB was setting party:true on ALL items. Now only vehicles and buildings get party:true — gear/weapons/armor/magic go to c.inv as intended.
Version 1.608 — Remove party debug logs. DB duplicates confirmed to be from a different user account (12080719…) — existing rows cleaned via SQL, push lock prevents future duplicates.
Version 1.607 — Fix duplicate party/squad rows: sbPush runs all operations in Promise.all (parallel), so concurrent pushes race — both delete, then both insert, creating duplicates. Added _pushInProgress lock so only one push runs at a time. Concurrent push attempts are silently dropped. Also cleans up stale duplicate rows already in the DB.
Version 1.606 — Fix: squads, characters, and icons showing stale localStorage data. Root cause: initSession was monkey-patched to call loadContentLibrary(false) immediately after starting, without waiting for sbPull to finish. This caused loadContentLibrary's internal renderAdv() to fire with stale S.squads from localStorage before sbPull had rebuilt S from the database. Monkey-patch removed — loadContentLibrary is already called inside initSession's sbPull chain.
Version 1.605 — Fix: renamed items not updating in store. loadItemsFromDB was merging DB rows over built-ins by name — so renaming an item created a duplicate instead of replacing the original. DB is now the sole source of truth: when DB rows exist for a category, they replace the built-in array entirely. Built-ins only serve as fallback when DB has zero rows.
Version 1.604 — Fix: franchise push was doing INSERT instead of UPSERT, causing a 409 duplicate key error on subsequent saves. Now uses UPSERT on user_id so the existing row is updated rather than re-inserted.
Version 1.603 — Items system: loadItemsFromDB() added. Fetches all published items from the items table (joined with effects via item_effects), merges into GEAR/VEHICLES/BUILDINGS arrays. Effects mapped to legacy item.effect format for full backward compatibility. Called on startup, login, and Pull.
Version 1.602 — Fix squad member count showing 0: loadContentLibrary was running in parallel with sbPull and calling renderAdv() before sbPull finished populating S.party. Now sbPull runs first, then content and pets load sequentially after, so S.party is always populated before any render.
Version 1.601 — Fix: SQUAD_ROLES and ROLE_KITS were accidentally deleted during pet migration (they lived between PETS and QUEST SETS). Restored.
Version 1.600 — Fix crash: BUILTIN_VEHICLES and BUILTIN_BUILDINGS were accidentally removed when BUILTIN_PETS was deleted during pet migration. Restored both snapshot variables so mergeCustomContent can reset vehicles and buildings to built-in state on each call.
Version 1.599 — Fix pets not showing in store: loadPetsFromDB was bailing early when uid() was null (session not yet restored, or guest). Now uses plain fetch with anon key — RLS already restricts to published=true for unauthenticated requests. Also called on guest boot.
Version 1.598 — Fix: pets not showing in store. loadPetsFromDB was never called on startup — only on manual Pull. Now called in initSession alongside sbPull and loadContentLibrary so pets load immediately on login.
Version 1.597 — Pet system migrated from JS array + content_items JSON to dedicated Supabase pets table. PETS is now loaded via loadPetsFromDB() from the pets table directly. No more BUILTIN_PETS, tombstones, or three-way merge logic. published column is a single boolean — true = in store, false = hidden.
Version 1.596 — Fix pet tombstone suppression: mergeCustomContent was filtering _deleted pets out of allPets before the suppression check could run, so tombstones never removed built-in pets from PETS. Fixed by running the suppression check first, then filtering. Also fixed editor: built-in pets now show as Published by default (they are in the store);
Version 1.595 — Fix: content_items visibility enforcement. loadContentLibrary was fetching ALL rows with no filter, so private content (e.g. unpublished pets) was visible to every user on every device. Now only fetches shared/official rows for everyone, plus private rows owned by the current user. Unpublishing an item now correctly hides it from other users' stores and sessions.
Version 1.594 — Content editor collapse/expand rebuilt with event delegation: single click listener on the list element handles toggle, reorder, and row selection in the correct priority order. Toggle no longer fights with row selection handler. Collapse state initialized after Supabase pull so custom cycles are visible. Quest reorder logic simplified.
Version 1.593 — Content editor full rebuild of save/load pipeline: (1) persistContentScope now uses upsert-by-content_key instead of delete-all + insert-all, eliminating the race condition that produced duplicate rows. (2) saveCustom simplified to a single call with no double quest-set save. (3) Quest scope resolution reads _visibility from Supabase row as ground truth. (4) After-save no longer does a full Supabase pull — in-memory state is already correct from upsert, so we just re-merge and re-render immediately. (5) Debug console.log noise removed from save handlers.
Version 1.592 — Content editor: cycle collapse toggle now works. Row click handler was firing even when the toggle button was clicked (stopPropagation was not preventing sibling listener on the same ancestor). Fixed by checking ev.target.closest() in the row handler and returning early for toggle/reorder button clicks.
Version 1.591 — Content scope system simplified for single-developer use. Replaced private/shared/official with Published/Unpublished. Published = shared (visible to all players); Unpublished = private (dev only). Scope dropdown removed from editor header. Each item now has a Published checkbox in its save row. All existing data preserved: private rows = unpublished, shared/official rows = published.
Version 1.590 — Content editor two-part fix: (1) openEditor now pulls from Supabase before rendering the list, so custom cycles appear immediately without needing a manual Pull. (2) Quest save scope now trusts _visibility stamped from the Supabase row as ground truth. Editor scope dropdown defaults to private for all users (admins can promote). Per-form scope selector pre-selects the quest's own scope. These together prevent custom quests from being saved to the wrong (official) bucket.
Version 1.589 — Quest save scope: _resolvedScope now re-reads the live quest object at click time (isBuiltin was a stale closure value from form-render time). Checks _visibility first (stamped from Supabase row visibility column), then only falls back to official for truly built-in content without a custom- setId prefix.
